LAW AND MOTION 302, HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF NICOLAS LAM'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE MATTER WAS NOT CONTESTED. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. CALIFORNIA'S COURTS "LIBERALLY PERMIT SUCH AMENDMENTS." (HIRSA V. SUP. CT. (1981) 118 CAL.APP.3D 486, 488-89.) NO TRIAL DATE IS SET. UNLESS NERISSA ZHANG IS AN ATTORNEY, SHE CANNOT LAWFULLY REPRESENT THE CORPORATE DEFENDANT. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER. CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA. DEPUTY: NOT PRESENT. MATTER NOT REPORTED. (302/RBU)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Coordinate Cases (Case No.
37-2024-00031047-Cu-Fr-Nc, San Diego)
Off Calendar Mar-07-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT NERISSA ZHANG's Motion To Coordinate Cases (Case No. 37-2024-00031047-Cu-Fr-Nc, San Diego)

NERISSA ZHANG's Motion To Coordinate Cases, is denied.
1. Defendant Zhang seeks to have this Court send the instant case to San Diego and coordinate it with the parties' San Diego action (Zhang v. Lam, Case No. 37-2024-00031047-CU-FR-NC).
2. This Court lacks the authority to issue such an order.
3. Under CCP 403, "[a] judge may, on motion, transfer an action or actions from another court to that judge's court for coordination." (See also CRC 3.500(h).)
4. If the defendant wishes to have this action transferred to San Diego, the motion needs to be made in the San Diego action.

Order is signed.

Visiting Judge: Barbara Zuniga, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/BZ)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-08-2024 Continued To Jan-29-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE
Off Calendar Oct-10-2024 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. DARCI BURRELL, Esq. of Burrell Kagin Law LLP (tel: 510-318-7700) for Plaintiff Nicolas Rosario Lam.
Defendant NERISSA ZHANG, in pro per present (tel: 415-549-6133).

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT NERISSA ZHANG's MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant Nerissa Zhang's "motion to change venue" is denied. A court in a county where any defendant resides when the action began is a "proper court for trial of the action" (CCP 395(a)). And defendant Bright Fitness Inc. stated in an official document signed by Zhang that its "principal executive office" was in San Francisco. (RJN Ex A.) Zhang's bare bones motion also refers to "convenience of the witnesses" and "existence of a related case," but fails to carry her burdens to demonstrate either. (Mot. 1:23-26; Tutor-Saliba-Perini Joint Venture v. Sup. Ct. (1991) 233 Cal.App.3d 736, 744 [movant's "burden of negativing plaintiff's choice of venue" with "competent evidence"].)

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/RBU)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Jun-13-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Nerissa Zhang, Tel. 415.549.6133, Defendant and Self-Represented Litigant;
No other appearance by any other party or counsel.

Hearing re: PLAINTIFF NICOLAS LAM'S Motion For Leave To File Amended Complaint.

The matter was not contested. Matter discussed. At the conclusion of discussions, the Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiff's motion for leave to file amended complaint is granted. California's courts "liberally permit such amendments." (Hirsa v. Sup. Ct. (1981) 118 Cal.App.3d 486, 488-89.) No trial date is set. Unless Nerissa Zhang is an attorney, she cannot lawfully represent the corporate defendant.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: Not present. Matter not reported.
(302/RBU)
